title: LVIS Plugin Manifest
description: Validates plugin.json manifest files for LVIS plugins (§9)

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL identifying the manifest schema version this plugin targets.

id.description: Plugin unique identifier. Kebab-case (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens). Min 3 chars — 2-char names reserved for future system namespaces. Example: "my-plugin".

description.description: Short human-readable summary used in the inactive-plugin catalog LLM sees in the system prompt.

version.description: Stable SemVer (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, no leading zeros). Pre-release / build-metadata suffixes are not supported on the marketplace publish channel — the per-plugin publish.yml rejects them at tag time. Aligning the schema with that gate so AJV fails earlier.

tools.description: Tool name list — underscore format required

allOf.$comment: kind="action" requires a tool field and forbids entry/exportName/page/window.
tool field is only allowed when kind="action".

tool.description: kind="action" 전용. 사이드바 아이콘 클릭 시 host 가 직접 디스패치하는 plugin tool 이름. 해당 tool 은 manifest.uiCallable[] 에 등록되어야 한다 — host 의 uiCallable 게이트가 그대로 enforce 됨. action 은 panel webview 를 *생성하지 않는다*.

window.description: Optional detachable-window defaults for this UI extension. When present the host creates a detached BrowserWindow for this view using these dimensions / snap hints. Not applicable to kind="action" (no window/panel is created).

width.description: Initial window width in DIP pixels

height.description: Initial window height in DIP pixels

defaultMode.description: Default window placement mode for the UI extension. 'embedded' renders inside main window; 'detached' opens in floating magnetic-snap window.

ui.description: UI extensions. Kind-specific required fields (embedded-module: entry+exportName; embedded-page: page; action: tool) are enforced at runtime with hard-fail — invalid entries fail manifest load, the plugin does NOT register. kind="action" entries never create a panel/window; the host directly dispatches the declared tool via api.callPluginMethod when the sidebar icon is clicked. The tool must be in manifest.uiCallable[]; runtime/index.ts:callFromUi enforces this gate. Cross-field validation (action ⇒ tool required + entry/exportName/page prohibited; non-action ⇒ tool prohibited) is encoded in the allOf block on each ui[] item.

capabilities.description: Closed vocabulary — see src/plugins/capabilities.ts (KNOWN_CAPABILITIES). Unknown values fail validation so typos don't silently grant nothing.

hostSecrets.description: Explicit allowlist of host-managed secrets the plugin reads via hostApi.getSecret. Currently allowed: llm.apiKey.* only. Default deny when unset.

read.description: Secret keys this plugin is permitted to read. Each entry MUST match ^llm\.apiKey\.[a-z]+(-[a-z]+)*$ — leading/trailing/consecutive dashes and other prefixes are rejected at validation time. Bounded (maxItems=32, per-item maxLength=64) to prevent registry-level DoS via oversized manifests.

startupTimeoutMs.description: Hard startup() timeout (ms). Exceeding plugins are fail-soft dropped. Default slow warning threshold is 5000ms.

uiCallable.description: Method allowlist invokable directly from renderer UI via lvis:plugins:call IPC. Methods not listed must go through ConversationLoop (permission / scope / expansion caps).

auth.description: Optional declarative auth contract. Lets the host render a generic 'authentication required / signed in' badge + login/logout button in Settings → 플러그인 설정 without baking plugin-specific auth code into the host. The plugin still owns its OAuth/cookie flow (see architecture.md §9.4a 'Plugin-Owned OAuth Authentication'); the host only invokes the named tools and listens for the auth-changed event. The three tool names referenced here MUST also appear in `uiCallable[]` (cross-field validated in manifest-validation.ts). On state transitions (login success, logout, token refresh failure) the plugin SHOULD emit an event named `<pluginId>.auth.changed` so the host UI can refresh without polling.

label.description: Human-readable label for the auth surface, shown next to the badge (e.g. 'Microsoft 계정', 'LGE 포털'). Defaults to the plugin's `name` if omitted.

statusTool.description: Tool name (must be in `uiCallable[]`) returning `{ authenticated: boolean, account?: string }`. Host invokes on Settings tab mount and after every `<pluginId>.auth.changed` event.

loginTool.description: Tool name (must be in `uiCallable[]`) the host invokes when the user clicks the '로그인' button. The plugin owns the actual auth flow (e.g. MSAL interactive, openAuthWindow); the host only triggers it.

logoutTool.description: Optional tool name (must be in `uiCallable[]` if present) the host invokes when the user clicks the '로그아웃' button. Omit to render a sign-in-only surface (e.g. plugins that don't support runtime sign-out).

partitionDomains.description: Hostnames the plugin may open inside its `persist:plugin-auth:<pluginId>` partition via `hostApi.openAuthPartitionViewer`. Treated as a dot-boundary suffix-match allow-list (e.g. `outlook.office.com` matches `mail.outlook.office.com` but not `outlook.office.com.attacker.com`). Schema rules: lowercase ASCII hostnames only, each entry MUST contain ≥1 dot (single-label hosts like `localhost` are rejected to avoid blanket subdomain matching), per RFC 1035 each label is bounded to 63 chars and the whole hostname to 253. Wildcards / URLs / port suffixes / whitespace / NUL / IDN-punycode labels (`xn--*`) are rejected. The host's load-time validator additionally rejects bare public suffixes (`com`, `co.kr`, `or.kr`, `go.kr`, `kr`, `net`, `org`, `io`, `ai`, `dev`, `app`); those rejections are mirrored here so manifest-publish surfaces them before runtime. Length cap 16 prevents an over-broad consent surface. The host re-validates this list at every viewer-open call.

bypassFocusGate.description: When true, this event bypasses the host focus-gate AND cooldown-gate suppression and always fires an OS-level notification regardless of LVIS window focus state or recent prior emits from the same plugin. The host additionally promotes urgent=true for bypass events (sound + critical urgency) unless the caller explicitly opts out. Use for critical flows (meeting capture, security alerts) where the user must be notified out-of-band. Refs lvis-project/lvis-app#843.

alwaysFireOs.description: When true, the host fires an OS-level notification *in addition to* the in-app toast even when the LVIS window is focused (dual delivery). The default focus-aware routing (focused → toast only, unfocused → OS only) still applies otherwise. Orthogonal to bypassFocusGate which forces OS-only routing and skips cooldown for critical flows; this field preserves toast delivery while adding OS visibility. Cooldown gate still applies. Use sparingly — opt-in per-event for high-importance plugin alerts that should reach the user even while they are actively in LVIS.

dependencies.description: Plugin dependencies that should be installed or considered with this plugin. This is not a delivery mode.

agentApprovalScopes.description: §8 P0 security — approval action scopes this plugin is permitted to issue and respond to via hostApi.agentApproval. Omitted or empty array means no approval scopes allowed (host enforces empty-list semantics). Known values: 'agent_file_share', 'agent_task_delegate', 'agent_external_api_call'.

requires.description: Runtime prerequisites declared by the plugin

llmKeySource.description: Whether this plugin uses host-managed LLM keys (host), its own keys (plugin), or no LLM at all (none). The 'host' source requires marketplace whitelist authorization at runtime.

packageName.description: npm package name persisted into installed manifests by PluginMarketplaceService for rollback support. Not authored by plugin developers.

icon.description: Optional Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. 'Mic', 'FileText'). Host dynamically looks up the named export from lucide-react and renders it as a monochrome stroke icon in the plugin grid popover. Unknown names fall back to the 'Plug' icon. Full list: https://lucide.dev/icons/

iconText.description: Optional short text (1-4 chars) rendered inside the plugin grid avatar — e.g. 'EP', 'MTG'. Takes precedence over `icon` when both are declared. Use when no Lucide glyph matches the plugin's domain identity.

python.description: Optional Python runtime co-deployment metadata. Host installs declared requirements when managedBy is lvis-app.

toolSchemas.description: LLM이 메서드를 호출할 때 사용하는 JSON Schema (draft-07)와 권한 정책 메타데이터. 키: method 이름, 값: { description, category, pathFields?, inputSchema }

category.description: Host permission policy category. 'meta' is host-only and cannot be declared by plugins.

pathFields.description: Input field names whose values are filesystem paths and must be checked against allowed directories. Dotted paths address nested object fields.

writesToOwnSandbox.description: Issue #664 P1 (lvis-app PR #860) — sandbox-write auto-LOW self-attestation. When true, every value resolved through pathFields must stay inside ~/.lvis/plugins/<pluginId>/. The host reviewer treats sandbox-local writes as LOW risk. The runtime canonicalizes and verifies the containment claim at invocation time — a tool that emits a path outside its own sandbox falls back to the standard write rules. Use for plugins that persist sandbox-local caches (e.g. MSAL token cache) without round-tripping the user.

version.description: §6.4 Tool versioning — optional stable SemVer (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, no leading zeros) for this tool. When omitted, the plugin manifest's top-level version is used. Same strictness as the top-level version field.

deprecatedSince.description: Stable SemVer (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) of the manifest version that deprecated this tool.

replacedBy.description: Tool name that replaces this deprecated tool.

configSchema.description: §9.2 Track B — declarative settings schema. JSON Schema draft-07 subset rendered as a typed form in PluginConfigTab. format:'secret' routes the value through the encrypted keychain instead of cleartext pluginConfigs.

default.description: Default value for the form field. Outer JSON byte-size cap is enforced server-side (see marketplace publisher.py); the schema bounds object/array primitives so a pathological default can't blow up the catalog.

author.description: Plugin author — individual maintainer name or contact (distinct from `publisher`, which is the publishing organization). Surfaced in catalog / IDE intellisense; not used for permission decisions.

uiSlots.description: Top-level advertisement of UI slot names this plugin participates in. Marketplace metadata only — actual extension binding lives in `ui[].slot`. Kebab-case identifiers, ≤32 entries.

allOf.$comment: auth contract requires a uiCallable allowlist — the three referenced tool names MUST also be in uiCallable[]; value-level cross-check is enforced in lvis-app's manifest-validation.ts
